DeepPrint

DeepPrint is a Deception Engineering tool for T-Pot / T-PotCE. It wraps
Cowrie, Conpot, and Suricata so a defender can re-skin a honeypot deployment
into a believable persona with customized hostnames, container names,
environment values, banners, injected web content, optional MAC identities,
and staggered startup timing.

Primary use cases
- Deploy OT / ICS / industrial deception personas on T-Pot
- Make a stock T-Pot install look more intentional and less generic
- Increase attacker friction with coherent service metadata and banners
- Bootstrap and manage persona swaps from a single Python CLI

Key files
- README.md: human-facing overview, quick start, deployment workflow
- DeepPrint/deepprint.py: main engine and CLI
- DeepPrint/CUSTOM_PERSONAS.md: how to create custom personas
- DeepPrint/templates/tpot.yml: bundled base Compose template
- DeepPrint/footprints/: built-in personas and assets

Important behaviors
- Uses bundled templates for rendering, even when targeting a live T-Pot root
- Writes generated files to docker-compose.deepprint.yml and .env.deepprint
- Backs up the pre-DeepPrint live config before activation
- Supports dry-run, interactive deploy, and restore workflows
- Uses subprocess for Docker and docker cp orchestration

Search terms
- deception engineering
- cyber deception
- T-Pot
- T-PotCE
- honeypot
- Cowrie
- Conpot
- Suricata
- ICS security
- OT security
- industrial control systems
- attacker friction
